common.tagline: A hybrid game of hidden actions and deduction

home.heroTitle: Move in plain sight. Act in secret.
home.heroSubtitle: RELIQUES, a hybrid game of hidden actions and deduction. Everyone has a secret mission on the same ancient site: first to complete theirs without being blocked wins.
home.feature1Text: You see where everyone goes, never what they do. The secret is in the what, never in the if.
home.feature2Text: Read the movements, collect info fragments, accuse at the right moment to block a rival.
home.feature3Text: Several missions target the same relic, with different verbs. You will get in each other's way.
home.badge: Hidden actions and deduction - 4 to 5 players
home.step1Text: The app secretly assigns you a three-step mission, anchored in specific locations.
home.step2Text: Move in plain sight, act in secret from your phone.
home.step3Text: Complete your public final act without being intercepted on the last turn.
home.finalText: Create your account, read the rules and jump into a game.

rules.title: RELIQUES
rules.tagline: A hybrid game of hidden actions and deduction
rules.pitchBody: An expedition digs through an ancient site. Each player secretly receives a mission: steal, forge, decode, unmask... Everyone moves in plain sight on the board, but acts in secret from their phone: you see WHERE each player goes and THAT they act, never WHAT. First to carry their mission through to its public final act, without being blocked, wins.
rules.hybridIntro: Two surfaces locked to each other: without the board, no public information to read, so no deduction; without the app, no secrecy and no resolution.
appItems: The fragments and key clues gathered
The true state of relics (real / fake / sabotaged)
rules.mapIntro: A site in three zones linked by bottlenecks: the Ruins (where the relics live), the Camp (base and objective locations), the Lands (extraction).
rules.mapNote: Several objective locations spread the action out: Extract (Docks or Cliff), Validate or forge (Workshop), Publish (Sanctuary), Offload (Bazaar). Cutting a bottleneck (Bridge, Rift) forces detours and reshapes the map mid-game.
rules.actionsIntro: One action per turn. Golden rule: you never act invisibly, only ambiguously. The secret is in the WHAT, never in the IF.

actions.name: Take / Drop a relic
actions.public: An action + the relic changes place

rules.constraintsNote: Inventory: 1 relic max. Theft and sabotage: only if you are in the same location as the target. Acting in silence avoids the Guardian but has a cost.
rules.missionsBody: A mission is not a ready-made recipe: its final act stays LOCKED until you have found a key clue. Often several relics look alike (real or fake): you must DISCOVER which one is the right one before acting, or your act lands on a fake and fails. You investigate to identify your target, then you act - and the others run the same investigation, in conflict. Shape: investigate -> target identified -> verb -> final location, plus a constraint (without being seen at the Temple, before round 8, with a fake...).

missions.name: The Thief
missions.steps: Three Idols on the site, only one real. Find which one (the Expert or by investigating) -> bring it to the Docks -> extract.

missions.name: The Forger
missions.steps: Forge the real relic (create a fake) -> have YOUR fake validated at the Workshop by the Expert.

missions.name: The Decoder
missions.steps: Gather three scattered fragments (Temple, Crypt, Gallery) that form a code -> open the vault -> publish at the Sanctuary.

missions.name: The Sleuth
missions.steps: Discover a rival's mission target (via the Witness or the Guardian) -> block them right at their final act.

rules.npcsIntro: Neutral characters populate the site; some of them move.

npcs.name: The Guardian
npcs.text: Patrols toward the LOUDEST action. If it reaches you after a noisy action, you risk being exposed (a fragment of your mission revealed) or dropping your relic. Silence avoids it, but it costs.

npcs.name: The Expert
npcs.text: Tells the real from the fake. Bring a relic to validate it... or to pass your fake off as real.

npcs.name: The Smuggler
npcs.text: At the Bazaar and the Docks: trade a relic, buy an info fragment, bribe.

npcs.name: The Witness
npcs.text: It is through them that you investigate a player: one attribute of their mission.

rules.sabotageIntro: Sabotage is spatial: you must be on site, and it acts on the geography.

sabotages.name: Trap a location
sabotages.text: The next one to act here springs the trap: loses a turn, drops their relic or leaks a fragment.

sabotages.name: Collapse a passage
sabotages.text: Cut a shortcut or a bottleneck (Bridge, Rift) for a few turns: forced detours, reshaped map.

sabotages.name: Forge on the spot
sabotages.text: Swap the relic for a fake: same public token, hidden state fake. Extracting it fails.

sabotages.name: Plant a false trace
sabotages.text: Lay a fake action token to mislead the others' deduction.

rules.turnBody: Turn by turn, rotating order. On your turn: 1) you choose 1 action on your phone; 2) the app resolves it and triggers the public effect (pawn moving, trace token + sound, relic changing hands, or announcement); 3) players update the board.
rules.climaxBody: Once your target is identified and you are at the right location, you declare your final act (Extract, Validate, Publish, Offload). The app announces it to all, but it only resolves at the START of your next turn: each opponent has exactly one turn to react (steal the relic, sabotage you, accuse you). Survive until your turn: you win.
rules.infoBody: The crux of it all, since missions run on locked information. One investigation = a single private fragment: on a trace -> the TYPE of the last action here; on a player (via the Witness) -> one attribute of their mission; on a relic (via the Expert) -> real or fake.
rules.accuseBody: A public action: you lay your token and you declare the target you believe is theirs. Right -> they drop the relic they carry and their intent is exposed. Wrong -> you reveal a fragment of YOUR mission. A real risk.
rules.endBody: First final act resolved without having been blocked = victory. Tempo safeguard: after about 12 to 14 rounds, the most advanced player wins (ties broken by correct accusations). At 4-6 players: 30 to 40 min.
